rules.rule: Freeze one bounded writable slice before implementation starts.
No concept lands without an owner, consumer, lifecycle, and proof surface.
Do not answer review criticism by expanding architecture outside the frozen slice.
Keep execution status, artifact status, review outcome, and terminal reason semantically distinct.
No frozen or ready claim is valid without adversarial verification and parity audit.
Role markdown, role contract, artifact matrix, completion clauses, and terminal payload must stay semantically aligned.
Declared governance history and audit artifacts are governed outputs and must not contradict effective terminal status or result payload semantics.
Prior artifacts may inform a run only as reference evidence unless the active authority package explicitly imports them as authority.

Invocation source refs may add run-scoped authority or baseline evidence, but canonical target semantics come from target-governance authority documents unless an explicit override policy is declared; conflicts fail closed.
Every mutable target surface must be bound to a declared writable-surface base version, and stale target mutations must fail closed.
Governance-routing records must be machine-readable and auditable, including affected paths and gatekeeper decision metadata for resolved proposals.
